claims.claim_text: The title claims LangChain "took over AI development."
claims.anchor_text: # LangChain Fundamentals: The Framework That Took Over AI Development
claims.suggested_rewrite: # LangChain Fundamentals: Building AI Applications with LangChain
claims.rationale: This is an unsupported superlative/adoption claim rather than a citable technical fact.

claims.claim_text: LangChain is an open-source framework for building LLM-powered applications and agents.
claims.rationale: The LangChain repository README directly describes LangChain as a framework for building agents and LLM-powered applications.

claims.claim_text: LangChain agents are built on top of LangGraph for more stateful control, including durable execution, streaming, human-in-the-loop behavior, and persistence.
claims.rationale: LangChain points readers to LangGraph for advanced orchestration, and the LangGraph README explicitly lists durable execution and human-in-the-loop/stateful workflow capabilities.

claims.claim_text: Python is the most common LangChain runtime for AI engineering teams.
claims.anchor_text: Python remains the most common LangChain runtime for AI engineering teams because the surrounding ML ecosystem is strongest there.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Python is a primary LangChain runtime because much of the surrounding ML ecosystem is available there.
claims.rationale: The comparative adoption claim is not directly backed by an allowlisted primary source here.

claims.claim_text: LangChain.js supports TypeScript/JavaScript runtimes such as Node.js, browsers, and edge/serverless environments.
claims.rationale: The LangChain.js README explicitly lists supported environments including Node.js, browser, Deno, Bun, and edge/serverless targets.

claims.claim_text: Older examples may have imports moved into provider-specific packages.
claims.anchor_text: LangChain packages move quickly, provider APIs change, and a working notebook from last quarter may use imports that have since moved into provider-specific packages.
claims.suggested_rewrite: LangChain packages move quickly, provider APIs change, and examples from older releases may no longer match the current package layout.
claims.rationale: The exact import-migration claim is too specific for the available allowlisted evidence, but the broader package-churn lesson is still valid.

claims.claim_text: Current LangChain deprecates legacy `LLMChain` patterns in favor of runnables/LCEL-style composition.
claims.span_hint: section: 3. Prompts, Parsers, and LCEL Composition
claims.rationale: The deprecation warning for `LLMChain` is documented in LangChain discussions, while the current repo quickstart centers the runnable/invoke model.

claims.claim_text: Session memory should be explicitly cleared or expired to prevent cross-session privacy leaks, especially in sensitive workflows.
claims.span_hint: section: 4. Memory, Retrieval, and State Boundaries
claims.rationale: OWASP MCP10 directly recommends ephemeral contexts, TTL/expiry, and context isolation to avoid cross-user and cross-tenant leakage.

claims.claim_text: Prompt injection can come from user input, retrieved documents, and tool outputs, so defenses must be layered rather than prompt-only.
claims.span_hint: section: 6. Production Patterns: Routing, Testing, Security, and Debugging
claims.rationale: OWASP documents direct injection, indirect injection, RAG poisoning, and agent/tool-specific attacks, along with layered mitigations.

claims.claim_text: LangChain began in late 2022 as an open-source project by Harrison Chase.
claims.rationale: The LangChain article states the project was launched in October 2022 by Harrison Chase.

claims.claim_text: The table classifies LlamaIndex as "RAG-first."
claims.anchor_text: | LlamaIndex | RAG-first | Private-company funding amount requires a source |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | LlamaIndex | Document and retrieval-oriented tooling | Private-company funding amount requires a source |
claims.rationale: The current official positioning has shifted, so the exact label is too narrow/outdated for a clean citation.

claims.claim_text: Haystack is focused on production-ready ML/LLM pipelines and is part of deepset.
claims.rationale: The official Haystack repository is under deepset-ai and describes Haystack as a production-ready orchestration framework for LLM applications.

claims.claim_text: AutoGPT is focused on autonomous agents.
claims.rationale: The official AutoGPT repository and org metadata explicitly frame it around agents/autonomous-agent tooling.

claims.claim_text: CrewAI is focused on multi-agent orchestration.
claims.rationale: The official CrewAI repository describes the framework as orchestrating collaborative AI agents and multi-agent systems.

claims.claim_text: Semantic Kernel is Microsoft-backed.
claims.rationale: The official repository is maintained under the `microsoft` GitHub organization.

claims.claim_text: LangChain has "10M+" monthly downloads.
claims.suggested_rewrite: | LangChain | Public package-install counts change over time | Ecosystem, integrations |
claims.rationale: The precise download statistic is time-sensitive and not directly backed by an allowlisted source here.

claims.claim_text: LlamaIndex has a "Large install base."
claims.anchor_text: | LlamaIndex | Large install base | RAG specialization |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | LlamaIndex | Public package-install counts change over time | RAG specialization |
claims.rationale: This is an unsupported market-adoption claim with no stable allowlisted source attached.

claims.claim_text: Haystack has "Established adoption."
claims.suggested_rewrite: | Haystack | Public package-install counts change over time | Production ML |
claims.rationale: This is an unsupported adoption claim rather than a verifiable source-backed statistic.

claims.claim_text: Semantic Kernel has "Established adoption."
claims.anchor_text: | Semantic Kernel | Established adoption | Microsoft integration |
claims.suggested_rewrite: | Semantic Kernel | Public package-install counts change over time | Microsoft integration |
claims.rationale: This is an unsupported adoption claim rather than a source-backed current metric.

claims.claim_text: CrewAI has "Established adoption."
claims.suggested_rewrite: | CrewAI | Public package-install counts change over time | Agent orchestration |
claims.rationale: This is an unsupported adoption claim rather than a verifiable statistic tied to an allowlisted source.

further_reading.title: LangChain GitHub Repository
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary upstream overview of LangChain’s scope, current architecture, and runnable-centric APIs.

further_reading.title: LangGraph GitHub Repository
further_reading.why_relevant: Best primary source for durable execution, human-in-the-loop, memory, and stateful agent workflow concepts referenced by the module.

further_reading.title: OWASP LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
further_reading.why_relevant: Grounded security reading for prompt injection, indirect retrieval attacks, and layered defenses in LLM applications.

notes: Ignored Mermaid diagrams, code blocks, quiz items, and shell exercises per instructions. Flagged table rows only where they make vendor-positioning or adoption/statistic claims.
